title: غزل شمارهٔ ۲۱
description: A compact Obeid Zakani ghazal of longing and impossibility: the speaker cannot leave the beloved's quarter, cannot endure separation, and insists that love, wine, and remembrance are inseparable from life.
full_text:
دگر برون شدنم زین دیار ممکن نیست
دگر غریبیم از کوی یار ممکن نیست

مرا از آن لب شیرین و زلف و عارض تو
شکیب و طاقت و صبر و قرار ممکن نیست

دلا بکوش مگر دامنش به دست آری
که وصل بی‌طلب و انتظار ممکن نیست

من اینکه عشق نورزم مرا به سر نرود
من اینکه می نخورم در بهار ممکن نیست

در آن دیار که ماییم حالیا آنجا
مسافران صبا را گذار ممکن نیست

عبید هم غزلی گاه گاه اگر بتوان
بگو که خوشتر از این یادگار ممکن نیست
translation:
To go out again from this land is not possible;
to be a stranger again to the beloved's lane is not possible.

Because of those sweet lips, that lock of hair, and your face,
patience, endurance, forbearance, and composure are not possible for me.

O heart, strive, perhaps you may seize the hem of the beloved,
for union without seeking and waiting is not possible.

For me not to love cannot enter my head;
for me not to drink wine in spring is not possible.

In that land where we now are,
passage is not possible even for the travelers of the morning breeze.

Obeid too, if from time to time he can manage a ghazal,
let him say that no keepsake sweeter than this is possible.

words.meaning: my estrangement; my being a stranger
I do not practice; I do not bear
it does not go; it does not pass
I will not be fooled / I will not eat
